summary: Gruber publishes his grades for Apple's 2018 performance across multiple categories, giving the Mac a harsh D driven primarily by the butterfly keyboard debacle and terrible Marzipan apps. iPhone earns the highest mark at A, with praise for the XS camera system and iOS 12's performance focus. iPad gets a B split between extraordinary hardware and stagnant software that desperately needs an 'iPadOS' overhaul. He adds an unofficial Retail category at C, criticizing the checkout experience and degraded Genius Bar service under Angela Ahrendts' leadership.
key_points: The butterfly keyboard situation is Apple's biggest Mac problem — they make a great standalone keyboard (Magic Keyboard 2) but refuse to put that quality in their laptops
iPhone XR is surprisingly competitive with the XS, and iOS 12 represents Apple at its software best by delivering on its promise to focus on performance
iPad hardware is years-ahead amazing but the software is stuck — the platform needs an 'iPadOS' overhaul that scales for advanced use without complicating simple use
Apple retail has lost its way on the fundamentals of shopping and service, with checkout now a maddening hunt for employees and Genius Bar appointments booked days out
The first-generation Marzipan apps are so bad they cost the Mac an entire letter grade
money_quotes: It used to be the case that Apple's notebook keyboards were widely hailed as the best in the world — that's no longer the case, and I think that's a problem.
I want you to take this keyboard and put it in these MacBooks.
After spending a few weeks using a XR full-time, I honestly question whether its LCD isn't better than the XS's OLED for my needs.
iOS 12 on iPhone is Apple at its software best.
The new iPad Pros are, quite simply, the best portable computers ever made by anyone. They feel like artifacts from a few years in the future.
The iPad needs to scale better for advanced use cases — without complicating simple use cases.
Part of the reason Apple's stores are too crowded is that people are wandering around trying to pay for things.
The primary purposes of an Apple Store should be shopping and service — and I think both of those experiences should be a lot better.
key_insight: Apple's 2018 was defined by a widening gap between exceptional hardware engineering and software and retail experiences that failed to keep pace.
